description: Stage-3a gate goldens — inline-suppression + baseline behaviors. These goldens document the full gate pipeline including suppression layers. The Rust engine must byte-match SARIF output after applying equivalent al-sem-ignore pragmas and baseline files.
capturePoint: analyzeWorkspace({deterministic:true}) → projectFinding/filterFindings → scope=primary → applyBaseline → applyInlineSuppressions → formatSarif/formatPrSummary
inlineSuppressionMatchingRule: // al-sem-ignore <detectorId>[: reason] suppresses a finding whose primaryLocation.line (1-based) equals directiveLine OR directiveLine+1. Same-line OR immediately-following-line (no blank line gap allowed). Keyed by (file, detectorId) — column-position-agnostic.
baselineByteStability: saveBaseline() pins generatedAt=new Date(0).toISOString()='1970-01-01T00:00:00.000Z'. fingerprints are sorted+deduped. With deterministic:true analysis, .baseline.json is fully byte-stable across runs. generatedAt is the ONLY volatile field (but it is pinned to epoch = non-volatile for these goldens).

stageB.antiDegenerate: full-baseline→0 results; partial-baseline≥1 result; round-trip byte-stable
